title_en: Taharah in Al-Mudawwanah: Maliki Purity Law at Its Source
title_ar: Taharah in Al-Mudawwanah: Maliki Purity Law at Its Source
content_en:
The chapters on taharah in Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra preserve the earliest systematic record of Maliki purity law as it emerged from the teaching of Imam Malik and was transmitted by his principal students. The question-and-answer format gives these chapters a distinctive character: they record real questions asked by students and the responses given by Ibn al-Qasim based on Malik's teachings.

On water, the Maliki school's approach differs from other schools in certain details. The Maliki school does not use the two-qullah threshold as a dividing line for standing water; instead, it distinguishes between water that is 'large' (kathir) and water that is 'small' (qalil) based on overall assessment, with large bodies of water resisting impurity and small amounts becoming impure through contact with najasah. The criterion for 'large' is not a fixed volume but a practical assessment based on whether the water could be described as a significant body. Al-Mudawwanah records Ibn al-Qasim's transmission of Malik's views on specific types of water — wells, cisterns, rivers, and pools — in the context of the questions asked.

On wudu, Al-Mudawwanah records the Maliki positions through Ibn al-Qasim's responses. The Maliki school requires wiping the entire head as an obligatory element of wudu — not merely part of it as the Shafi'i school holds. This position, based on the Maliki reading of the Quranic verse on wudu, is one of the school's characteristic distinctions. The question format of Al-Mudawwanah reveals how this position was developed: Ibn al-Qasim explains that Malik consistently required wiping the full head and that this was the practice he observed in Medina.

The Maliki school's non-requirement of sequence (tartib) in wudu — departing from the Shafi'i and Hanbali requirement — is reflected in Al-Mudawwanah. Ibn al-Qasim explains that while the Quran mentions the wudu elements in a specific order, this order is not understood by Malik as obligatory, and washing the feet before the face, for example, does not invalidate the wudu as long as all the obligatory elements are performed.

On ghusl, Al-Mudawwanah records the Maliki positions on the minimum and complete forms. The Maliki school requires that the ghusl include dalk (rubbing) of the body as an obligatory element — a position that distinguishes it from the other three schools, which treat rubbing as merely recommended. Ibn al-Qasim explains this based on Malik's consistent instruction and the practice he observed.

The question-and-answer format of Al-Mudawwanah preserves not only the positions but the texture of early Maliki legal reasoning, showing how scholars worked through questions by reference to the practice they had observed in Medina and the reports they had received from Malik.
title_id: Taharah dalam Al-Mudawwanah: Hukum Kesucian Maliki dari Sumbernya
content_id:
Bab-bab taharah dalam Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra melestarikan rekaman sistematis paling awal dari hukum kesucian Maliki sebagaimana muncul dari pengajaran Imam Malik dan ditransmisikan oleh murid-murid utamanya. Format tanya-jawab memberikan jendela langsung ke dalam cara para ulama awal bekerja melalui pertanyaan-pertanyaan praktis, dan ke dalam cara Imam Malik sendiri mempertimbangkan berbagai kondisi sebelum menetapkan posisinya.

Dalam pembahasan tentang air, posisi Maliki memiliki beberapa kekhasan dibandingkan mazhab lain. Malik dikenal dengan pandangannya bahwa air sedikit pun tidak menjadi najis hanya karena bersentuhan dengan benda najis, selama tidak berubah warna, bau, atau rasanya. Pendekatan praktis ini mencerminkan pemikiran Maliki yang cenderung memudahkan (taysir) dalam hal-hal yang menyulitkan masyarakat umum.

Pembahasan tentang wudhu dalam Al-Mudawwanah memperlihatkan perdebatan internal mazhab Maliki yang sangat hidup. Ibn al-Qasim terkadang meriwayatkan dua pendapat dari Malik yang tampak kontradiktif, yang mencerminkan perkembangan pemikiran Malik atau kondisi yang berbeda di mana setiap pendapat diberikan. Sahnun dengan cermat merekam kedua pendapat dan terkadang memberikan penjelasan tentang kondisi mana yang berlaku untuk masing-masing.

Mandi janabah, haid, nifas, dan isu-isu berkaitan dengan kesucian perempuan mendapat perhatian yang sangat rinci. Pembahasan Imam Malik tentang kondisi-kondisi yang membolehkan atau mewajibkan mandi mencerminkan perhatiannya yang besar terhadap kemudahan dalam praktek ibadah sehari-hari. Al-Mudawwanah menjadi rujukan utama bagi para ulama Maliki di Afrika Utara, Afrika Barat, dan Andalusia dalam memutuskan pertanyaan-pertanyaan taharah yang praktis.
